title: Household Production
paragraphs: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is a measure that reflects the overall economy of a country. It represents the total final transaction value of goods and services produced within a country or a specific place over a certain period. This includes all private and public consumption, government spending, investment, inventories, construction costs, as well as import and export costs and income. In Nepal, GDP is calculated annually. According to Trading Economics, Nepal's GDP for 2023 was approximately 40 trillion (NPR), with a per capita income of 1,399 US dollars.
